Catalog page states this double-counting restriction applies to all four second-introductory-course options, not just POLI1021: POLI1021 (How to Rule the World) may satisfy Political Theory, POLI1061 (Intro to American Politics) may satisfy American Politics, POLI1081 (Intro to International Politics) may satisfy International Politics, and POLI1091 (Intro to Comparative Politics) may satisfy Comparative Politics — but in each case 'They may not...satisfy the Introductory requirement and the subfield requirement at the same time.' COMPLETENESS GAP (not modeled): catalog page states subfield requirements specifically must be satisfied by courses taken in the department — courses taken abroad or transferred from other institutions may only be used to fulfill introductory or elective requirements, not subfield requirements. This older cohort has no separate FORM4900 Senior Seminar requirement. Old-cohort total is also 30 credits (12+9+9); re-verified by independent re-fetch on 2026-07-15.

totalCredits (30) is computed by summing the stated per-component credit figures for the Class of 2028+ cohort (9 core + 3 senior seminar + 18 electives); the department page lists these component credit totals but does not print one explicit major-total line. Re-verified by independent re-fetch on 2026-07-15: confirmed no explicit major-total figure appears on the page for either cohort, and confirmed the arithmetic (9+3+18=30) is correct.
Only 2 courses may be double-counted toward the TES major and the Psychological Humanities minor, per the department page.
FORM2252 was renamed 'Forms of Language and Life'; students who previously took FORM2252 under its old title should not retake it despite the name change (department page note).

The department page labels its elective list a 'Sample List of Electives' and states not all courses are offered every semester, directing students to EagleApps for current offerings — the pool may not be fully exhaustive.

No separate 'Lynch/LSEHD common core' sits under this major (unlike CSOM's common core). TES majors instead complete the university-wide Boston College Core Curriculum plus the major-specific requirements modeled below; no second major or minor is required for this major, per both source pages.

AUDIT (2026-07-15, independent re-fetch of both cited pages): (1) REMOVED an unsupported parenthetical '(stated as 42 credits)' previously attached to the BC Core Curriculum mention above — that figure does not appear on either cited source page and could not be verified; only the university catalog page's generic '120 credits' full-degree figure was found, which is unrelated to this major specifically. (2) Confirmed via re-fetch that the Class of 2028+ core list (FORM1050/1051/2050/2051, choose 3 of 4), senior seminar (FORM4900, 3 credits), and elective pool (all 19 FORM-code items listed below) match the department page's 'Class of 2028 and beyond' section exactly, and that the broader APSY/EDUC elective pool belongs only to the 2025-2027 cohort's separate elective list (confirmed by locating APSY2041 specifically within the 2025-2027 section only). (3) COMPLETENESS GAP (not fixed, flagged for review): only the Class of 2028+ structure is modeled as queryable requirementGroups; the Class of 2025/2026/2027 structure (which governs the majority of currently-enrolled TES students as of catalogYear 2026-2027) exists only as unstructured prose in this notes array, not as its own structured entry/requirementGroups — a consumer reading only requirementGroups would not see it. Flagging as needs-review for this reason; no other material errors found in the modeled Class of 2028+ data.
